entities.description: retrieval augmented generation is a system framework described as operating in a two phase process
offline indexing phase is the first phase where unstructured corpus data is organized into a structured index
online retrieval phase is the second phase where the system retrieves relevant components based on a user query
vector databases are mentioned as a possible form of structured index in the offline indexing phase
kg  knowledge graph  is mentioned as a possible form of structured index in the offline indexing phase
llm is the component that generates output informed by retrieved components in the online retrieval phase
user query is the input used in the online retrieval phase to retrieve relevant components
document s native tree topology is the logical structure that the proposed approach seeks to integrate with retrieval structures
unstructured corpus data is the input material organized into a structured index during the offline indexing phase
text chunks are examples of relevant components retrieved during the online retrieval phase
subgraphs are examples of relevant components retrieved during the online retrieval phase
the document is the source of the original logical hierarchy and native tree topology referenced in the text

relations.description: retrieval augmented generation systems operate in the offline indexing phase as their first step
retrieval augmented generation systems operate in the online retrieval phase as their second step
vector databases are a form of structured index created during the offline indexing phase
kg is a form of structured index created during the offline indexing phase
the online retrieval phase uses the user query to retrieve relevant components
the online retrieval phase informs the llm s generation with retrieved components
the proposed approach for retrieval augmented generation seeks to integrate retrieval structures with the document s native tree topology
the offline indexing phase organizes unstructured corpus data into a structured index
text chunks are retrieved as relevant components during the online retrieval phase
subgraphs are retrieved as relevant components during the online retrieval phase
the retrieval augmented generation approach seeks to integrate structures with the document s native topology
